meta.title: Agentic AI Security — MCP Proxy Gateway & Tool Call Interception | Data Science Technologies
meta.description: Production-ready agentic AI security: MCP proxy gateway, tool call interception, chain tracking, and multi-agent orchestration security with published benchmarks.
meta.keywords: Agentic AI Security, MCP Security, MCP Proxy Gateway, AI Agent Security, Tool Call Interception, Chain Tracking, Multi-Agent Orchestration, AI Tool Authorization, Agent Rate Limiting

hero.title: Agentic AI Security
hero.subtitle: Production-ready security for AI agents, tool calls, and multi-step workflows

intro: AI agents that can browse the web, execute code, call APIs, and manage infrastructure need security controls that match their capabilities. ForceField provides the only production-ready security gateway with quantitative benchmarks for agentic AI.

mcp_gateway.title: MCP Proxy Gateway
mcp_gateway.description: ForceField includes an MCP proxy gateway that sits between AI agents and tools. Every tool call is evaluated against security policies before execution. The gateway supports Claude Desktop, Cursor, and custom agents via the MCP protocol. Chain state is backed by a pluggable store (in-memory or Redis) for multi-instance deployments.
features: Transparent proxy: agents connect to ForceField instead of tools directly
Policy evaluation before every tool call execution
Support for MCP protocol (Claude Desktop, Cursor, custom agents)
Pre-call authorization and post-call result inspection
Configurable per-agent, per-tool policies

tool_interception.title: Tool Call Interception
tool_interception.description: Every tool call passes through ForceField's security pipeline before reaching the target system.

policies.name: Deny
policies.detail: Tool call blocked, agent receives policy violation message

policies.name: Require Approval
policies.detail: Tool call held for human review before execution

policies.name: Rate Limit
policies.detail: Per-agent, per-tool token bucket rate limiting

policies.name: Schema Validate
policies.detail: Tool inputs validated against expected schemas

policies.name: Result Inspect
policies.detail: Tool outputs scanned for PII, secrets, and injection

policies.name: Sandbox
policies.detail: Risky tools executed in isolated OpenShell containers instead of blocked

chain_tracking.title: Chain-of-Action Attack Detection
chain_tracking.description: ForceField monitors multi-step agent workflows and detects attack patterns that span multiple tool calls.

patterns.name: Data Exfiltration
patterns.detail: Agent reads sensitive data then attempts to send it externally

patterns.name: Reconnaissance
patterns.detail: Agent systematically probes available tools and permissions

patterns.name: Rapid Tool Calls
patterns.detail: Abnormal burst of tool calls indicating automated attack

multi_agent.title: Multi-Agent Orchestration Security
items: Trust levels assigned per agent identity
Delegation management: which agents can invoke other agents
Policy-enforced per-agent tool allowlists and trust boundaries
Per-agent rate limiting with configurable windows

instruction_integrity.title: Zero-Trust Intent Validation
instruction_integrity.description: Every inter-agent delegation is cryptographically verified. ForceField uses HMAC-SHA256 signing with nonce replay protection to ensure tool call instructions have not been tampered with in transit between agents.
features: HMAC-SHA256 instruction signing at every hop
Content hash verification detects tampered arguments
Nonce replay protection prevents instruction reuse
Provenance tracking records the full delegation chain
Advisory and enforce modes for gradual rollout

lateral_movement.title: Lateral Movement Prevention
lateral_movement.description: ForceField binds agent identities to authenticated sessions and bearer tokens. If an agent attempts to spoof another agent's identity, hijack a session, or escalate trust levels, the request is blocked and the event is recorded.
features: Session-agent binding with bearer token verification
Identity spoofing detection across sessions
Trust escalation prevention (agents cannot re-register at higher trust)
Cross-session attack detection
Real-time Prometheus metrics for all lateral movement events

sandbox_execution.title: OpenShell Sandbox Execution
sandbox_execution.description: ForceField integrates with NVIDIA OpenShell to provide graduated tool enforcement. Instead of binary allow/deny decisions, risky tools like shell execution and file deletion are routed into isolated containers with scoped filesystem, network, and process constraints. If chain pattern detection identifies a data exfiltration or privilege escalation sequence, the sandbox's network policy is tightened in real time -- cutting outbound access before data can leave.
features: Graduated enforcement: risky tools run in sandboxes instead of being blocked
On-demand container isolation per agent and session
Dynamic egress restriction when suspicious chain patterns are detected
Default-deny network policy with ForceField gateway as the only allowed inference endpoint
Prometheus metrics for sandbox execution, duration, and egress restrictions

cta.title: Secure Your AI Agents
cta.description: Request access to ForceField and deploy production-ready agentic security.
